opp.name: Easa Saleh Al Gurg Group_P2P_2026
opp.next_step: 10% confidence 26th May- Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues- no compelling need at present- follow up end June 12th Feb- initial interest from Group CIO (known to Zycus). Appears as potential replacement of Coupa P2P Will be in Dubai 25th to meet F2F
opp.ais_why:
• Across only 154 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single dated entry from 12 Feb 2026 ‚Äî there is no AI mention. The entire NextStep reads: 'initial interest from Group CIO (known to Zycus). Appears as potential replacement of Coupa P2P. Will be in Dubai 25th to meet F2F.' This is a Coupa P2P replacement framing, not an AI deal.
• Product scope is eProcurement; eInvoicing; Merlin; Merlin Intake. Merlin and Merlin Intake are bundled but the captured engagement is at 'initial interest' stage with a single F2F meeting promised. BD_Engagement is Unengaged. There has been no demo, no RFI, no AI-specific dialogue ‚Äî just a Group CIO contact and an upcoming Dubai meet.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is extremely thin (154 chars, one note). The Coupa P2P replacement framing plus Unengaged BD plus 10% confidence in SFDC all point to early-stage; with Merlin/Merlin Intake bundled but absent from conversation, score 3 reflects the AI products being a Zycus value-add rather than a buyer demand.

sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam Anand: 'P2P solution...get rid of Opentext...vendor portal where vendors can come in and upload invoicing...basic controls like 3 way match'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Dubey: 'centralization of procurement across diverse business units...shift to a full shared-services model'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Carl Kimball (Zycus): 'customers have seen a 60 to 70% reduction in TCO' (Zycus claim, not buyer-confirmed value)
Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'no compelling need at present' (absence of quantified compelling event)
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated operational pain but has not quantified savings, ROI, or cost impact. On the 19 Mar 2026 call, Gautam Anand (Group Head IT) stated the primary need is P2P with integrated vendor portal and three-way matching to replace the current OpenText invoicing system, which is 'not implemented very effectively' and lacks a vendor portal where vendors can upload invoices. Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group CIO, Decision Maker contact role) added that the group plans centralization of procurement across diverse business units with a future shift to a full shared-services model. Carl Kimball (Zycus) mentioned that Zycus iSaaS middleware promises '60-70% TCO reduction' for SAP integration effort, but the buyer has not confirmed their own value case, cycle-time improvement target, headcount reduction, or savings goal. The deal has stalled before any ROI workshop or quantified business case was co-built. The freeze stated on 26 May ('no compelling need at present') signals that quantified urgency was never established. Next move: when re-engaging, co-author a quantified value case (OpenText licensing cost elimination, vendor-portal cycle time, AP headcount, error-rate reduction) with the procurement and finance leads.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: only 2 buyer-side roles (Subodh, Gautam); both IT, neither procurement nor business-unit leadership
Avoma calls 18 Feb, 5 Mar, 19 Mar 2026: only Subodh and Gautam attended from buyer side across all three touchpoints

Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'this is a preliminary call...we don't know what solution is the right fit...we realized Coupa might not be the right fit, similar case with Ariba' (evaluative, skeptical)
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'we will internally discuss...propose time slots in April' — proposed next step never actioned
Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'Project is on freeze...no compelling need at present' (no internal advocacy to overcome freeze)
Zero buyer-initiated contact since 19 Mar 2026 (102 days ago)
No tasks, introductions, or documents shared by buyer; no multi-threading to procurement or business stakeholders
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The relationship is IT-led and IT-confined, with no evidence of internal advocacy, multi-threading to procurement or business stakeholders, or active deal-pushing from the buyer side. Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group CIO, Decision Maker contact role) and Gautam Anand (Group Head IT, Executive Sponsor contact role) are the only two buyer participants across all three calls (18 Feb intro, 5 Mar overview, 19 Mar overview). Both are friendly and engaged during calls—Subodh led strategic discussion and Gautam managed logistics—but their behavior is evaluative and cautious, not advocacy. On 19 Mar Subodh stated 'this is a preliminary call...wanted to understand how the solution is...we don't know what solution is the right fit' and explicitly cited past failures with Coupa and Ariba, signaling skepticism rather than conviction. He proposed the next step ('we will internally discuss...propose time slots in April') but never followed through, and the project froze on 26 May with no compelling need. There is no evidence of either Subodh or Gautam driving the deal internally (no tasks logged by them, no procurement-team intro, no business-stakeholder intro, no follow-up after the 19 Mar call). They have not shared internal documents, introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer or procurement leadership, provided requirements, or pushed back on competitive alternatives. The freeze itself—and the absence of any buyer-initiated re-engagement after the stated June follow-up date—indicates they are not championing this deal. The relationship is single-threaded to two IT contacts with no access to procurement (the pain owner), finance (budget authority), or business-unit leadership (shared-services adoption). No coach has been identified. Next move: when re-engaging, explicitly ask Subodh and Gautam whether they will advocate for Zycus internally, and request introductions to the Group Head of Procurement (the Head of Supply Chain contact on the account is listed as Pramod Sakhare, not confirmed as involved in this opp) and a business-unit procurement lead to build a multi-threaded coalition and develop a true champion at the operational pain-owner level.

sources: Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues—no compelling need at present' (Do Nothing is the current state)
Next_Step__c 12 Feb 2026: 'potential replacement of Coupa P2P'

Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'we used SAP Ariba for many years till last year...replaced with Coupa a couple of months back...we realized Coupa might not be the right fit...similar case with Ariba'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'reaching even 80% fit is easier said than done...challenge we face with Coupa and Ariba...how flexible and customizable your solution is'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Carl (Zycus): 'iSaaS...60-70% reduction in TCO...app-extend...70-80% fit, configuration into the 90%'
No other P2P vendor named in any call, Next_Step, or Task; no active bake-off or RFP
competition.narrative: The primary competitive threat is Do Nothing / project freeze, with Coupa and SAP Ariba as the context (past evaluations, both rejected, but shaping buyer skepticism). On 19 Mar 2026 Subodh stated the group used SAP Ariba for sourcing, vendor onboarding, and contract management 'for many years till last year' and replaced those modules with Coupa 'a couple of months back' (early 2026). He said 'when we got into the onboarding...we realized this might not be the right fit for us. Similar case we had with the relevant stakeholder and Coupa are out of active consideration for P2P (Coupa was implemented only for sourcing/vendor onboarding, not P2P, and is already judged a poor fit). The Next_Step dated 12 Feb states the opportunity 'appears as potential replacement of Coupa P2P,' but Subodh's 19 Mar comments clarify Coupa is not in use for P2P and is already failing on the modules they did implement, so Coupa is faded/dismissed rather than an active P2P rival. The real competition is inertia and the geopolitical freeze: Next_Step 26 May states 'Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues—no compelling need at present.' The buyer is not actively evaluating any other named P2P vendor as of the last call (19 Mar), and no RFP, shortlist, or bake-off has been run. The Ariba/Coupa experience has made the buyer cautious and skeptical of SaaS platforms ('reaching 80% fit is easier said than done'), raising the bar for Zycus to prove configurability and fit. The freeze has lasted 99 days with no sign of thaw. Zycus wins by re-engaging with a quantified,fit-validated, urgency-driven business case that directly addresses the Coupa/Ariba configurability concern and ties to a forcing event (OpenText contract end, ERP transformation, budget window). The wedge is Zycus iSaaS (60-70% TCO reduction for SAP integration, a differentiated claim vs Coupa/Ariba), Merlin intake (AI-driven adoption, demonstrated on the call and well-received by the agentic-AI-piloting buyer), and app-extend configurability (Carl's pitch on 19 Mar: 'we don't do heavy customizations to core...app-extend allows us to build functionality outside core...70-80% fit, configuration into the 90%').

sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam: 'using OpenText for invoice management, which I would say is not implemented very effectively...do not have a vendor portal where vendors can come in and upload invoicing...looking to get rid of Opentext'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'procurement is distributed...we intend to get into a centralized model...big change...need the technology which can support that'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'we realized might not be the right fit...similar case with Ariba...each business has different dynamics'
Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'no compelling need at present' (pain exists but urgency does not)
identify_pain.narrative: The buyer has clearly articulated operational pain with their current invoicing and procurement setup, owned by IT and procurement leadership. On 19 Mar 2026 Gautam Anand stated they are using OpenText for invoice processing, which is 'not implemented very effectively,' and lacks a vendor portal, forcing manual invoice intake and creating inefficiency. He said 'we do not have a vendor portal where vendors can come in and upload the invoicing,' and the requirement is 'get rid of Opentext' and implement a quick P2P solution with vendor portal and three-way match integrated with the relevant stakeholder Dubey added that the group's procurement is currently distributed across diverse business units, creating inefficiency and lost leverage, and the vision is to centralize into a shared-services model to consolidate commodity spend (he gave the example of steel and furniture, which multiple business units purchase independently). He stated 'we intend to get into a centralized model...it's a big change for the organization...we need the technology which can support that.' Additional pain: past negative experience with Coupa and Ariba—Subodh said 'we realized might not be the right fit for us. Similar case we had with Ariba'—indicating frustration with inflexible SaaS platforms that cannot accommodate the group's complexity and leading to his explicit concern about reaching 80% requirements fit. The pain owner is IT (Subodh, Gautam) driving the platform search, but the operational pain sits with procurement and AP teams (not yet engaged). The freeze stated on 26 May ('no compelling need at present') suggests the pain, while real, is not acute enough to overcome the geopolitical distraction or drive urgency. Next move: when re-engaging, quantify the pain (OpenText cost, manual AP cycle time, error rate, procurement fragmentation cost) with the procurement and finance teams, and tie it to a forcing event (OpenText contract renewal, ERP transformation window, budget cycle) to create urgency.

sources: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel discussion in any Avoma call or Next_Step
Ground-truth note: 'this looks PARTNER-LED' but no partner named in evidence
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec, and partner involvement have not been discussed. The deal froze at preliminary discovery before any commercial or contract conversation. The buyer is a UAE-based conglomerate (real estate, construction, retail, distribution, manufacturing); typical procurement in this region and deal size may require MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, and potentially CFO or group-executive sign-off, but none of this has been mapped. No mention of current contract expiration, replacement timeline forcing function, or procurement-process governance. No SI/partner role identified (the note in the ground truth states 'this looks PARTNER-LED and/or APAC' and instructs to treat sparse Avoma coverage as normal for a partner-led deal, but no partner entity or individual has been named in any call, contact role, or task). Next move: when re-engaging, ask who manages vendor contracts and legal review (in-house legal counsel vs procurement vs finance), what the InfoSec and data-privacy review entails for a SaaS P2P platform integrated with SAP, whether an MSA is required or a simple Order Form suffices, and what the signature authority threshold is for a $400k commitment.

economic_buyer.narrative: Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group CIO) holds the Decision Maker contact role and appeared on all three Avoma calls (intro 18 Feb, overviews 5 Mar and 19 Mar 2026), leading the buyer-side discussion and articulating strategy (centralized procurement, shared services, ERP transformation). His authority over IT spend and procurement-platform decisions is assumed given his title and active sponsorship role, but commercial and budget authority has not been confirmed—no pricing, budget cycle, CFO involvement, or approval-chain conversation has occurred. On 19 Mar Subodh stated 'we will internally discuss...then propose time slots in April' for a detailed workshop, signaling control over the evaluation timeline, but he has not been tested on commercial terms, ROI sign-off, or multi-approval requirements. Gautam Anand (Group Head IT, Executive Sponsor contact role) is the stated point of contact and co-led the 19 Mar call, but his role appears operational/technical rather than commercial. The deal froze before any Economic Buyer validation (budget holder, CFO, procurement executive) was surfaced. Next move: when re-engaging, map the commercial authority chain—who approves a $400k SaaS spend, whether CFO/finance sign-off is required, and whether Subodh alone controls the decision or escalates to group executive leadership.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running remains informal and unstructured; no evaluation timeline, approval gates, or vendor-selection method has been defined. The 19 Mar 2026 call was an introductory solution overview (the third touchpoint: intro 18 Feb, overview 5 Mar, overview 19 Mar, all not recorded except the last). Subodh stated 'this is a preliminary call...we will have another call along with our business team, procurement and the project team...probably in April' for a detailed demonstration and requirements workshop, and 'post break we will...go through the detailed demonstration.' The buyer was in evaluation mode for Coupa (recently implemented sourcing/vendor onboarding modules 'a couple of months back' per Subodh, but found it 'might not be the right fit') and had previously used SAP Ariba for sourcing/vendor onboarding until 2025. The P2P layer is a net-new evaluation. The planned April workshop was the gate to advance from preliminary interest to formal evaluation, but it never occurred. The Next_Step dated 26 May states the project is frozen with no compelling need and a 'follow up end June' instruction; that June follow-up has not happened as of 29 Jun. No approval chain, committee structure, CFO gate, or vendor-shortlist process has been discussed. The call sequence shows IT-led discovery (Subodh and Gautam only) with procurement and business stakeholders conspicuously absent despite the stated intent to involve them. The decision process is stalled at preliminary discovery with no path forward. Next move: when re-engaging, map the formal process—vendor shortlist count, evaluation committee (IT + procurement + business + finance), demo/workshop schedule, RFP/RFI gate if any, CFO approval requirement, contract/legal timeline, and a mutual close plan with dated milestones.

sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam: 'P2P solution...implemented very quickly...vendor portal...3 way match...integrated with SAP ECC'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'reaching even 80% fit is easier said than done...each business has different dynamics...how flexible and customizable your solution is'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'ERP transformation...second half of this year, 12-18 month'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh: 'we already started on agentic AI...pilots...work in progress'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, buyer-proposed action: 'deep-dive workshop...two to three hours...in April...with business team, procurement and project teams'
No RFP, BRD, or formal evaluation criteria document shared
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has stated high-level must-haves but has not run a formal evaluation, shared an RFP/BRD, or validated detailed fit. On 19 Mar 2026 Gautam specified P2P with vendor portal, three-way match, and SAP ECC integration as baseline requirements, and Subodh emphasized configurability and fit, stating 'in my experience...reaching even 80% of this kind of standard configuration product is easier said than done...each business has different dynamics...that's the challenge we face with Coupa and Ariba.' He asked 'how flexible and customizable your solution is' and noted the group's diversity (projects, retail, distribution, manufacturing, real estate) as a fit challenge. Carl (Zycus) acknowledged '70-80% fit...with configuration into the 90%' and offered to map requirements in detail. The buyer proposed a 'two- to three-hour deep-dive workshop...in April...with business team, procurement and project teams' to validate fit and demonstrate use cases, but that workshop was never scheduled. Additional stated criteria from the call: quick implementation (Gautam: 'a solution which can be implemented very quickly'), SAP S/4HANA roadmap compatibility (Subodh: 'ERP transformation...second half of this year, 12-18 month exercise'), AI/GenAI capability (the group is piloting agentic AI for sales and customer service, and the Merlin intake demo was well-received), and a vendor-portal UX that drives adoption across a diverse, multi-entity conglomerate. Procurement and business-unit stakeholder criteria (the actual end-users) remain unknown—Subodh and Gautam are IT, not procurement. The freeze before the workshop means formal decision criteria are unconfirmed. Next move: when re-engaging, co-author the evaluation scorecard with IT and the procurement lead, map it to a live Zycus config session, and get written sign-off on fit before advancing stage.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Curious trending toward AI Hungry. The buyer is actively piloting agentic AI for other functions (sales, customer service) and engaged positively with the Zycus AI demo on 19 Mar 2026, indicating strategic appetite. However, they have not yet tied AI capability to procurement ROI or made it a decision criterion, so the signal is Curious (interested, exploring) rather than Hungry (AI is a must-have, driving urgency). The freeze suggests AI alone is not compelling enough to overcome inertia. Elevate AI to Hungry by quantifying the Merlin intake adoption ROI (cycle-time reduction, compliance lift, user satisfaction for a multi-entity rollout) and the agentic AI headcount-optimization case (autonomous AP matching, invoice processing, vendor onboarding) in the re-engagement business case.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 12 Feb 2026 with initial interest from Group CIO Subodh Dubey as a potential Coupa P2P replacement. Three touchpoints Feb-Mar 2026 (intro 18 Feb, overviews 5 Mar and 19 Mar). On 19 Mar buyer proposed April workshop with business/procurement teams to map detailed requirements; workshop was never scheduled. Next_Step dated 26 May states project frozen due to geopolitics, no compelling need, follow-up end June. The June follow-up has not occurred. Zero buyer activity since 19 Mar 2026 (102 days ago). Stage Qualified since creation; no stage progression. Last modified 29 Jun 2026 (data update, not deal movement).

They have not driven the deal internally, introduced Zycus to procurement or business stakeholders, or overcome the freeze. 102-day silence and the failure to schedule the proposed April workshop prove they are not championing this opportunity.

items.action: Re-engage Subodh and Gautam to assess whether the geopolitical freeze has lifted and whether a compelling event now exists.

If the freeze persists with no restart date or forcing event, recommend disqualify or move to Omitted forecast and long-nurture cadence.

items.expected_effect: Create urgency by quantifying the cost of inaction and tying the business case to a forcing event (OpenText contract renewal, ERP transformation H2 2026 window, FY budget cycle). A quantified, finance-validated ROI is the only lever to overcome the 'no compelling need' freeze.

items.expected_effect: De-risk another 'not the right fit' rejection by proving configurability and fit in a live session before investing further sales effort. Fit validation is a gate to advance; without it, the deal will repeat the Coupa/Ariba failure pattern.

items.action: Position Merlin intake as the adoption and UX solution for a complex multi-entity P2P rollout and tie it to the agentic AI pilots the buyer is running for sales and customer service.

items.expected_effect: Elevate AI from a feature to a strategic differentiator and ROI driver. Make Merlin intake the centerpiece of the multi-entity rollout story (ease of adoption, policy compliance, user satisfaction) and quantify the agentic AI headcount-optimization case (autonomous AP matching, invoice processing). This directly addresses the buyer's strategic AI interest and differentiates from Coupa/Ariba.

No champion developed; no internal advocacy or deal-pushing from buyer side
Requirements fit unvalidated; no RFP, BRD, or formal evaluation criteria shared; proposed workshop to validate fit never occurred
No commercial, pricing, or budget discussion; Economic Buyer authority assumed but not confirmed
confidence_signals.summary: Deal has gone cold. Buyer proposed April workshop on 19 Mar 2026 but never scheduled it. Project explicitly frozen 26 May with no compelling need. Planned June follow-up did not occur. Zero buyer-initiated contact for 102 days. Single-threaded to two IT contacts with no procurement or business stakeholder engagement. No champion, no fit validation, no commercial discussion. The freeze, silence, and lack of follow-through on stated next steps all signal the deal is off track and the buyer is not prioritizing it.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 Feb 2026 (138 days ago). Stage Qualified since creation. Last verified buyer activity 19 Mar 2026 (102 days ago, last recorded call). Next_Step__c dated 26 May states project frozen due to geopolitics, no compelling need, follow-up end June. Today is 29 Jun; the planned June follow-up has passed with no movement logged. Days to close: 105. Time in Qualified: ~138 days. A Qualified-stage deal 105 days from close with a 102-day buyer silence and an explicit freeze is Off Track.
evidence: Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues- no compelling need at present- follow up end June'
LastActivityDate 19 Mar 2026 (102 days ago, matching final Avoma call 19 Mar)
No Task, Event, or Avoma activity since 19 Mar 2026

Stage Qualified; close date 12 Oct 2026 (105 days out); forecast Pipeline with 10% confidence annotation in Next_Step
Buyer explicitly stated on 19 Mar call: next session probable in April with business team; April came and went with no workshop scheduled
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal frozen since 26 May 2026 due to buyer-stated geopolitical freeze with no compelling need; 102 days with zero buyer engagement, no restart date, and October 2026 close date not credible.

flags: Single-threaded to IT, zero procurement engagement: Only 2 contact roles, both IT (Subodh Kumar Dubey, Group CIO; Gautam Anand, Group Head IT). Buyer stated on 19 Mar call intent to involve 'business team, procurement and project teams' in next session, but no introductions made in 99 days. P2P is a procurement-led initiative; IT alone cannot close this. If deal unfreezes, demand introductions to Head of Procurement (Pramod Sakhare, Head of Supply Chain, is an account contact but not engaged on this opp) and business unit stakeholders as condition for continued pursuit.

No fit validation despite buyer's explicit 80% fit concern: Subodh stated on 19 Mar call: 'my experience says that we always struggle to get to 80%... each business has different dynamics... that's the challenge we face with Coupa and Ariba.' This is the critical evaluation criterion. Buyer proposed April deep-dive workshop to map detailed requirements; never scheduled. If deal unfreezes, make the requirements fit workshop (2-3 hours with procurement and business teams, detailed use-case demos, BRD mapping) the rank-1 action and prerequisite to any further pursuit. Without it, Zycus cannot prove differentiation vs Coupa.

No compelling event or quantified urgency despite real operational pain. The freeze ('no compelling need at present') proves pain is not acute. Quantify the OpenText cost, AP cycle time, and procurement-fragmentation waste with finance and procurement, and tie re-engagement to a forcing event: OpenText contract renewal date, ERP transformation H2 2026 window, or FY budget cycle.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a frozen, single-threaded, IT-only engagement with no champion, no procurement stakeholder access, no fit validation, and no urgency. The buyer explicitly stated skepticism about SaaS fit based on Coupa/Ariba failures. The deal will not advance without a multi-threaded relationship, a quantified business case co-built with procurement and finance, and a forcing event to create urgency.

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is Do Nothing / project freeze (active since 26 May 2026, 34 days and counting, with no restart date or compelling event to overcome inertia). Coupa and SAP Ariba are both out: Ariba was replaced in 2025, and Coupa was implemented early 2026 for sourcing/vendor onboarding only and already judged a poor fit by the buyer on 19 Mar 2026. Neither is in active P2P consideration. The buyer is not running a live vendor bake-off. The real competition is buyer inertia, geopolitical distraction, and SaaS-platform skepticism (the Coupa/Ariba experience has made them cautious about fit). Zycus was the only P2P vendor engaged as of 19 Mar, but the freeze and 102-day silence mean the opportunity to win by default has been lost to inaction. Re-engagement must address the freeze with a quantified, urgency-driven business case.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: we realized might not be the right fit for us...reaching even 80% of this kind of standard configuration product is easier said than done...each business has different dynamics
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa is out of P2P consideration (only sourcing/vendor onboarding implemented, and already failing). Use the Coupa failure as proof that Zycus app-extend configurability and iSaaS integration (60-70% TCO reduction vs Coupa's standard connectors) directly address the fit and flexibility concerns the buyer raised. Demonstrate a live config session mapping their complex multi-entity requirements to prove 80%+ fit before advancing stage.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: we used SAP Ariba for many years till last year...similar case we had with Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba was replaced in 2025 and is not in consideration for P2P. The buyer's stated dissatisfaction with both Ariba and Coupa (inflexibility, poor fit for complex conglomerate) is a wedge for Zycus, but only if Zycus can prove superior configurability and integration in a live requirements workshop. Do not assume the win—validate fit explicitly.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Freeze
competitors.quote: Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues—no compelling need at present—follow up end June
competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage with a quantified business case (OpenText cost elimination, AP cycle-time savings, procurement-centralization ROI) tied to a forcing event (OpenText contract renewal, ERP transformation H2 2026, budget cycle). Offer a light, fast-start pilot (vendor-portal quick win) to create momentum and prove fit without requiring full commitment during the geopolitical uncertainty.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and well-matched to the buyer's strategic interest. The buyer is piloting agentic AI for sales and customer service (Subodh stated on 19 Mar 'we already started on some use cases...creating a platform for agentic AI...did some pilots, work in progress'), and the Zycus Merlin intake demo (AI-driven conversational procurement interface in Microsoft Teams) and agentic AI platform overview were well-received on the 19 Mar call. Carl (Zycus) positioned Zycus as 'AI first company...wake up every day, all we talk about with customers is AI...automate and optimize and drive savings' and demonstrated intake management and agentic AI workflows. The buyer did not object or express skepticism about AI capability; the engagement on AI was positive. However, AI positioning has not been converted into a differentiated win strategy or urgency driver because the deal froze before commercial or fit validation occurred. The AI story is strong but under-leveraged—it should be the centerpiece of re-engagement (Merlin intake as the adoption/UX solution for a complex multi-entity rollout, agentic AI as the headcount-optimization ROI lever) but is not yet positioned as the reason to choose Zycus over doing nothing.

items.quote: reaching even 80% of this kind of standard configuration product is easier said than done...each business has different dynamics...how flexible and customizable your solution is
items.criterion: Requirements fit and configurability for a complex, multi-entity conglomerate (projects, retail, distribution, manufacturing, real estate)

items.quote: looking for a quick solution which can be implemented very quickly, get rid of Opentext
items.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam Anand; Carl (Zycus) stated '5 to 7 months typical P2P implementation timeline'
items.criterion: Quick implementation timeline (buyer wants to move fast, replace OpenText quickly)

items.quote: we will be starting our ERP transformation journey probably the second half of this year...12 to 18 month exercise...go to S/4 for all businesses
items.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh; Zycus iSaaS positioned as ECC and S/4HANA compatible with 60-70% TCO reduction claim
items.criterion: SAP ECC integration now, S/4HANA compatibility for H2 2026 transformation (12-18 month ERP project)

items.quote: we already started on some of the use cases. We are creating a platform for agentic AI for sales, customer service...we did some pilots, work in progress
items.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh; Zycus Merlin intake and agentic AI platform demonstrated and well-received
items.criterion: AI and agentic AI capability (buyer is piloting agentic AI for sales and customer service)

items.quote: do not have a vendor portal where vendors can come in and upload the invoicing...get rid of Opentext...vendor portal...3 way match...integrated with SAP
items.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam; Zycus P2P includes vendor portal and three-way match as standard
items.criterion: Vendor portal with self-service invoice upload and three-way match to replace OpenText

items.quote: we intend to get into a centralized model...complete shared services model for procurement...leverage certain commodity product...it's a big change...need the technology which can support that
items.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh; Zycus platform is multi-entity capable and Carl cited similar conglomerate customers
items.criterion: Support for procurement centralization and future shared-services model across diverse business units

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expectations are high and skepticism is entrenched due to past Coupa and Ariba failures. The buyer explicitly stated 'reaching even 80% fit is easier said than done' and asked 'how flexible and customizable your solution is.' Zycus positioned app-extend configurability, iSaaS SAP integration, and Merlin intake AI adoption as differentiators on the 19 Mar call, but fit has not been validated through a requirements workshop or live config session. The planned April deep-dive never occurred, so expectations remain unmet and the deal froze.

hard.ais_why:
• Across only 154 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single dated entry from 12 Feb 2026 ‚Äî there is no AI mention. The entire NextStep reads: 'initial interest from Group CIO (known to Zycus). Appears as potential replacement of Coupa P2P. Will be in Dubai 25th to meet F2F.' This is a Coupa P2P replacement framing, not an AI deal.
• Product scope is eProcurement; eInvoicing; Merlin; Merlin Intake. Merlin and Merlin Intake are bundled but the captured engagement is at 'initial interest' stage with a single F2F meeting promised. BD_Engagement is Unengaged. There has been no demo, no RFI, no AI-specific dialogue ‚Äî just a Group CIO contact and an upcoming Dubai meet.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is extremely thin (154 chars, one note). The Coupa P2P replacement framing plus Unengaged BD plus 10% confidence in SFDC all point to early-stage; with Merlin/Merlin Intake bundled but absent from conversation, score 3 reflects the AI products being a Zycus value-add rather than a buyer demand.

Offer a light, fast-start pilot (vendor-portal quick win) to create momentum and prove fit without requiring full commitment during the geopolitical uncertainty.

packets.subject: Do Nothing / Freeze

value.flag: Dark for 99 days: Last buyer engagement 19 Mar 2026 (solution overview call with Subodh and Gautam). Next Step from 26 May states 'Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues- no compelling need at present- follow up end June.' That June follow-up date has passed with no activity. Immediate action: re-engage Subodh/Gautam to confirm whether geopolitical situation has stabilized and whether P2P initiative has been unfrozen; if yes, restart with April workshop; if no, qualify out or push close date to 2027.

packets.subject: Dark for 99 days: Last buyer engagement 19 Mar 2026 (solution overview call with Subodh and Gautam). value.flag: Single-threaded to IT, zero procurement engagement: Only 2 contact roles, both IT (Subodh Kumar Dubey, Group CIO; Gautam Anand, Group Head IT). Buyer stated on 19 Mar call intent to involve 'business team, procurement and project teams' in next session, but no introductions made in 99 days. P2P is a procurement-led initiative; IT alone cannot close this. If deal unfreezes, demand introductions to Head of Procurement (Pramod Sakhare, Head of Supply Chain, is an account contact but not engaged on this opp) and business unit stakeholders as condition for continued pursuit.

packets.subject: Single-threaded to IT, zero procurement engagement: Only 2 contact roles, both IT (Subodh Kumar Dubey, Group CIO; Gautam Anand, Group Head IT). value.flag: No fit validation despite buyer's explicit 80% fit concern: Subodh stated on 19 Mar call: 'my experience says that we always struggle to get to 80%... each business has different dynamics... that's the challenge we face with Coupa and Ariba.' This is the critical evaluation criterion. Buyer proposed April deep-dive workshop to map detailed requirements; never scheduled. If deal unfreezes, make the requirements fit workshop (2-3 hours with procurement and business teams, detailed use-case demos, BRD mapping) the rank-1 action and prerequisite to any further pursuit. Without it, Zycus cannot prove differentiation vs Coupa.

gaps: Only 1 of 3 buyer touchpoints was recorded and transcribed (19 Mar 2026); the intro call (18 Feb) and first overview (5 Mar) were not recorded, so early discovery content and any competitive/requirements discussion from those calls is unknown.

No procurement, business-unit, finance, or Economic Buyer stakeholder has been engaged or appeared on any call despite buyer's stated intent on 19 Mar to involve them in the next session. Stakeholder map is incomplete and single-threaded to IT.
No RFP, BRD, or detailed requirements document shared; proposed April workshop to map requirements and validate fit never occurred, so formal decision criteria and fit analysis are unconfirmed.
No commercial, pricing, budget, or CFO discussion has occurred across any call, Task, or Next_Step; Economic Buyer authority and approval chain are assumed but not validated.
Restart date and compelling event to overcome the freeze are unknown; June follow-up (per 26 May Next_Step) did not occur, and no subsequent outreach or status update has been logged.
